scope: Implement Web P0 Prisma schema, migration, seed, local DB strategy, and P0 model readback tests only.

commands.evidence: Initial Prisma 7 failures were fixed by adding prisma.config.ts, removing datasource url from schema.prisma, regenerating after adapter install, and using explicit Prisma Client adapter construction.
Local SQLite database apps/web/t03-local.sqlite was created/synced non-destructively from apps/web/prisma/schema.prisma.
Installed Prisma 7 SQLite adapter dependencies required for direct Prisma Client readback.

commands.command: pnpm.cmd run install in better-sqlite3 with workspace npm_config_cache/npm_config_devdir
commands.evidence: Native binding build first failed on sandbox-denied user/D:\tmp cache paths; final build passed using .pnpm-store/native-cache under the workspace.
Seed initially failed on Prisma 7 client adapter and CJS import issues; final seed inserted anon_web_seed_001 and generated an AuraCard fixture.
Generated apps/web/prisma/migrations/20260604010930_init/migration.sql against a separate local SQLite migration DB.
Final readback verified all 10 P0 models, counts, fixture IDs, and draw/generate/save/seal idempotency keys.
tsc --noEmit passed after Prisma helper additions.

localDbStrategy.productionTarget: PostgreSQL + Prisma remains the product target from the PRD/spec.
localDbStrategy.t03Validation: SQLite via Prisma 7 and @prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3 was used for local, non-destructive, sandbox-safe schema/seed/readback validation.
localDbStrategy.note: Generated *.sqlite files are ignored; durable proof is the readback JSON and command log.

rerunResumeState.notes: Use pnpm.cmd and --config.store-dir=.pnpm-store on this Windows surface. If better-sqlite3 binding is missing after a clean install, run its install script with npm_config_cache and npm_config_devdir under .pnpm-store/native-cache.

exitCondition: T04-T07 can use @auracue/web Prisma schema, generated client, and repository helpers to implement /api/v1/*; T03 did not implement API routes or later tasks.
